SEARCH
Package com.example.eventlottery.models

Class Registration

java.lang.Object
com.example.eventlottery.models.Registration
public class Registration extends Object
Role: Data Model / Entity Purpose: Represents the relationship between a User and an Event within the lottery system. It tracks the specific state of a user's application (e.g., waiting, selected, or cancelled) and stores the geographical coordinates of the entry if the event requires geolocation. Design Pattern: Data Transfer Object (DTO). This class facilitates the mapping of Firestore documents into local Java objects. Encapsulates the participation data for a user in a specific event, including their current lottery status and registration metadata.

  • Constructor Details

    • Registration

      public Registration()
      Default constructor required for Firebase Firestore deserialization. Initializes the status to "waiting" and sets the joined timestamp to the current time.

    • Registration

      public Registration(String eventId, String userId, String userName, String userEmail)
      Constructs a new Registration with essential event and user details.
      Parameters:
      eventId - The unique ID of the associated event.
      userId - The unique ID of the user registering.
      userName - The display name of the user.
      userEmail - The email address of the user.

  • Method Details

    • getId

      public String getId()
      Returns:
      The unique Firestore document ID.

      id - The unique Firestore document ID to set.

      public String getStatus()
      Returns:
      The current lottery status (e.g., "waiting", "selected").

    • setStatus

      public void setStatus(String status)
      Parameters:
      status - The new lottery status to set.

    • getJoinedAt

      public long getJoinedAt()
      Returns:
      The epoch timestamp when the user joined the waiting list.

    • setJoinedAt

      public void setJoinedAt(long joinedAt)
      Parameters:
      joinedAt - The join timestamp.

    • getRespondedAt

      public long getRespondedAt()
      Returns:
      The epoch timestamp when the user responded to an invitation.

    • setRespondedAt

      public void setRespondedAt(long respondedAt)
      Parameters:
      respondedAt - The response timestamp.

    • getLatitude

      public double getLatitude()
      Returns:
      The latitude coordinate of the user's entry.

    • setLatitude

      public void setLatitude(double latitude)
      Parameters:
      latitude - The latitude coordinate.

    • getLongitude

      public double getLongitude()
      Returns:
      The longitude coordinate of the user's entry.

    • setLongitude

      public void setLongitude(double longitude)
      Parameters:
      longitude - The longitude coordinate.

    • isGeoVerified

      public boolean isGeoVerified()
      Returns:
      True if the registration has been verified via geolocation.

    • setGeoVerified

      public void setGeoVerified(boolean geoVerified)
      Parameters:
      geoVerified - The geolocation verification status.

    • isHasGeolocation

      public boolean isHasGeolocation()
      Alias for isGeoVerified to support legacy code callers.
      Returns:
      True if geolocation data is present and verified.

    • setHasGeolocation

      public void setHasGeolocation(boolean v)
      Alias for setGeoVerified to support legacy code callers.
      Parameters:
      v - The geolocation verification status.
